name: azure-monitor-query-py
description: Query Azure Monitor logs and metrics using the official Azure Monitor Query SDK for Python with Log Analytics and Metrics Query clients
capabilities: Generate Kusto queries for Log Analytics workspaces
Query Azure Monitor metrics with aggregation options
Handle batch queries for multiple workspaces
Convert query results to pandas DataFrames
Use async clients for non-blocking operations
Filter metrics by dimensions and time ranges
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This skill contains documentation and code examples for the Azure Monitor Query SDK for Python. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of executable code, resulting in a risk score of 0/100. The skill is prompt-only with no executable code, containing reference documentation for querying Log Analytics workspaces and Azure Monitor metrics. Environment variable access is documented for Azure authentication but poses no security risk as these are standard Azure SDK configuration patterns.
valueStatement: Developers struggle to query Azure Monitor data efficiently without proper SDK guidance. This skill provides ready-to-use code examples for Log Analytics and Metrics Query clients with authentication patterns.
userTitle: Query Azure Monitor logs and metrics with Python SDK
limitations: Requires valid Azure credentials and workspace configuration
Does not execute queries directly - provides code examples only
Limited to Azure Monitor data sources, not other Azure services
Requires the azure-monitor-query Python package to be installed

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Monitoring Application Health
useCases.description: Query application request rates, error counts, and response times from Log Analytics to create dashboards and alerts for production monitoring

useCases.title: Data Analyst Investigating Performance Issues
useCases.description: Run Kusto queries to identify slow requests, exceptions, and bottlenecks in application performance data stored in Azure Monitor

useCases.title: Cloud Architect Analyzing Resource Utilization
useCases.description: Query Azure Monitor metrics to analyze CPU usage, network traffic, and resource consumption patterns across Azure infrastructure

promptTemplates.title: Basic Log Query
promptTemplates.prompt: Write a Kusto query to count AppRequests grouped by ResultCode for the last hour using LogsQueryClient in Python
promptTemplates.scenario: Beginner - Simple query to get started with Log Analytics

promptTemplates.title: Time Range Query
promptTemplates.prompt: Query Log Analytics for AppExceptions over a specific date range from January 1-2, 2024 using Python with proper timezone handling
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate - Working with specific time windows

promptTemplates.title: Metrics with Aggregations
promptTemplates.prompt: Query Azure Monitor metrics for CPU percentage with Average, Maximum, and Minimum aggregations using MetricsQueryClient with 5-minute granularity
promptTemplates.scenario: Intermediate - Combining metrics with multiple aggregation types

promptTemplates.title: Batch Query Multiple Workspaces
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute batch queries across multiple Log Analytics workspaces to retrieve AppRequests and AppExceptions simultaneously, handling partial results and errors gracefully
promptTemplates.scenario: Advanced - Production-ready batch operations with error handling

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for secure authentication that supports multiple credential types
Set appropriate time ranges and granularity to limit data volume and improve query performance
Handle partial results and failures gracefully using LogsQueryStatus checks
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode workspace IDs or resource URIs - always use environment variables
Avoid querying without time bounds as this can return excessive data and incur high costs
Do not ignore partial results - always check LogsQueryStatus before processing response

faq.question: What Azure permissions do I need to query Log Analytics?
faq.answer: You need Log Analytics Reader role on the workspace or higher permissions. For metrics, you need Reader access to the resource.

faq.question: How do I authenticate with Azure Monitor Query SDK?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically tries multiple authentication methods including managed identity, Azure CLI, and environment credentials.

faq.question: What is the cost of querying Log Analytics?
faq.answer: Log Analytics charges per GB of data scanned. Optimize queries with time ranges, filters, and projection to reduce costs.

faq.question: Can I query multiple workspaces in a single request?
faq.answer: Yes, use the batch query feature with LogsBatchQuery objects to run multiple queries efficiently in one API call.

faq.question: What time formats does the SDK accept?
faq.answer: Use Python timedelta for relative times (eg, last hour) or datetime objects with timezone info for absolute time ranges.

faq.question: How do I handle async operations with the SDK?
faq.answer: Use the aio module clients (LogsQueryClient, MetricsQueryClient) with async/await syntax and remember to close clients and credentials.
